articles.title: Comedian Matt Buechele's New Podcast Promises Laughs, Absurdity, and Sharp Takes on Modern Life
articles.summary: What do you get when a viral comedian with nearly 1 million followers launches a weekly video podcast? 'That Sounds Like a Lot,' premiering May 1 with guest Zach Zimmerman. Matt Buechele's new show will unpack culture, politics, and the 'increasingly strange rhythms of modern life.'

articles.title: Inflation Is Erasing Teacher Pay Raises, Leaving Educators Earning Less Than a Decade Ago
articles.summary: A 3.5% raise sounds good, right? Not when inflation is factored in. A new NEA report reveals that U.S. public school teachers have seen their real earnings drop by nearly 5% since 2017, despite average salaries rising to $74,495.

articles.title: In War-Torn Gaza, a Local Election Offers a Glimmer of Political Hope
articles.summary: Amid devastation and displacement, Deir al-Balah in central Gaza held its first election in over 20 years. Turnout was just 23%, but Palestinian officials call it a crucial step toward long-delayed presidential elections and eventual statehood.
